title: System Overview
content: Rolnopol is a comprehensive, multi-featured agricultural management platform designed for farmers, administrators, and superadmins. It enables secure management of farms, resources, users, and financial operations across multiple business domains. The system features: real-time farm resource management (fields, animals, staff), peer-to-peer marketplace for resource trading, integrated financial tracking with virtual currency, real-time multi-user messaging with WebSocket support, commodity portfolio management, weather data integration with regional alerts, event-driven notification system, AI-powered chatbot assistance, and robust administrative controls with feature flags and system monitoring. Built for collaborative workflows, the platform combines traditional agricultural management with modern financial and trading capabilities.

content.description: Represents a system user (farmer, admin, or superadmin) with authentication, profile, and role information. Manages relationships (friends, blocked users) and social interactions.
A plot of land owned by a user. Fields have a name, area, location/district info, and can have staff or animals assigned. Fields are core tradeable assets.
Livestock or other animals owned by a user. Includes type, quantity, and optional field assignment. Animals are core tradeable assets.
Human resources (workers) associated with a user's farm. Includes demographics and field assignments. Staff can be assigned to fields for work management.
Links staff to fields, representing work assignments and resource allocation on the farm.
Tracks a user's virtual currency (ROL) balance, transaction history (income/expense), and financial operations.
Represents an offer to buy or sell a resource (field or animal) in the peer-to-peer marketplace. Tracks offer status (active, sold, cancelled, unavailable).
Records a completed marketplace transaction including buyer, seller, asset, price, and timestamp for audit trail and financial reconciliation.
Investment commodity (gold, silver, platinum, crypto, etc.) that users can buy, hold in portfolio, and sell for speculative trading.
Direct message between users including sender, recipient, content, timestamp, and read status for real-time communication.
A conversation thread between two users with message history and metadata (participants, last activity).
Regional or user-specific alerts covering weather, irrigation, disease, pests, soil conditions, machinery, livestock, and logistics events.
System event emitted by application for notification processing, with event type, payload, and dispatch status tracking.
Processed event resulting in a notification delivered through channels (in-app, webhooks) with delivery status and destination.

Can manage their own farm, resources, assignments, and transactions. Can buy and sell on the marketplace. Access to premium features depends on feature flags. ⚠️ Some permissions below marked with ⚠️ are feature-flagged and may not be available.
permissions: Register and log in
Manage own profile and farm data
Add/edit/delete fields, animals, staff, and assignments
View and create marketplace offers for resources
Buy/sell resources from the peer-to-peer marketplace
View and manage own financial account with transaction history
Transfer virtual currency to other users
View weather forecasts and regional alerts ⚠️ (feature-flagged: weather, alerts)
Buy and sell commodities with portfolio tracking ⚠️ (feature-flagged: commodities)
Send/receive direct messages with other users ⚠️ (feature-flagged: messenger)
Access AI chatbot for farming assistance ⚠️ (feature-flagged: assistant)
Manage social connections: add friends, block users
Download financial reports (PDF/CSV exports)
View system health and status information

content.description: Oversees and manages resources and financial data for all users. Access to administrative dashboards, system monitoring, and operational controls. ⚠️ Can manage feature flags to control availability of flagged features.
permissions: All user permissions
View and manage all users' resources (fields, animals, staff)
View all financial accounts and transaction history
View all marketplace offers and transactions
Manage feature flags (enable/disable features)
Access administrative dashboards and overview statistics
Manage user accounts (activate/deactivate/delete)
Access audit logs and system health checks
Backup and restore database state
Moderate messaging system (mute/unmute users, delete messages)
Control commodities market (enable/disable trading, set limits)
View system metrics and performance data
Export application logs

steps: User navigates to the registration or login page.
To register, user provides a unique email, displayed name, and password.
System validates the input and creates a new user account.
After successful registration, the user is automatically logged in and redirected to their dashboard.
For login, user enters email and password and receives an authentication token upon success.
Authentication token is returned in the response and set as a cookie ('rolnopolToken'). Login time is also set as a cookie ('rolnopolLoginTime').
Authentication token is required for subsequent API requests to access protected resources. It can be sent as a cookie or in the Authorization header (Bearer token).
Session expiration: 24 hours for users, 1 hour for admin.
If login fails (invalid credentials, deactivated account, or validation error), user receives an error message and can retry.
Passwords are stored in plain text (for demo purposes only; not recommended for production).
Cookies are accessible to client-side JavaScript (not httpOnly).
Rate limiting is applied to login attempts.
Logout endpoint clears authentication cookies and invalidates the session.
After login, user is redirected to /profile.html.

error_cases.message: User with this email already exists

content.summary: Peer-to-peer trading of farm resources (fields, animals) between users with financial settlement.
steps: User A navigates to the marketplace section to view available offers.
User A can filter, search, and browse offers for fields and animals by price, type, and seller.
To sell, User A selects an unassigned resource they own and creates a marketplace offer with pricing.
System checks: asset is not assigned to any staff/field and not already offered.
If valid, offer is created and listed as 'active' in the marketplace visible to all users.
User B browses marketplace offers, finds User A's offer, and initiates a purchase.
System validates: User B is not the seller, has sufficient funds, and the asset is still available.
Upon approval, virtual currency is transferred from buyer to seller.
Asset ownership is transferred to User B in the database.
Financial transactions are created for both buyer and seller.
Offer status is updated to 'sold' and a marketplace transaction record is created.
Users can view their marketplace transaction history with all trades and pricing.

content.summary: Comprehensive management of farm assets, workforce, and operational planning.
steps: User accesses the farm management dashboard from their profile.
User views comprehensive farm overview: all fields, animals, and staff.
To add a field: user provides name, area, and optional location/district data. Field is created and listed in inventory.
To add animals: user specifies type (cows, goats, sheep, etc.), quantity, and optional field assignment.
To hire staff: user enters worker demographics and field assignments. Staff is added to the farm team.
Assignments management: user assigns staff to fields for work coordination or animals to fields for grazing.
Rule enforcement: assigned assets cannot be listed on marketplace (marked 'unavailable' if attempted).
User editing: complete CRUD operations for all resources with real-time inventory updates.
User deletion: remove resources with validation (assigned resources cannot be deleted directly).
Search and filtering: locate specific resources by name, type, or assignment status.
Pagination support for farms with large resource inventories.

content.summary: Comprehensive financial management with virtual currency, transactions, and reporting.
steps: User view their financial account showing current balance in ROL (virtual currency).
System displays all transactions: income (from sales, transfers) and expenses (purchases, transfers).
To transfer funds: user selects another user and amount, enters confirmation, transfer is executed. Both parties receive transaction records.
Each marketplace sale automatically creates financial transactions adjusting both parties' balances.
Users can filter and search transaction history by date, amount, type, and counterparty.
To generate reports: user exports financial statements to PDF or CSV for record keeping.
User views financial statistics: total income, expenses, balance trends, and marketplace activity.
System prevents overdraft: users cannot spend more than their balance.
Admin can view all users' financial accounts for auditing and oversight.

content.summary: Direct peer-to-peer communication with real-time delivery and message history.
steps: User opens messaging interface and views list of existing conversations.
To start new conversation: user selects another user to message.
User types and sends a message, WebSocket connection delivers it in real-time to recipient.
Recipient receives notification and can see message immediately (if online) or upon next login.
Message history is maintained: users can scroll through previous conversations.
Read receipts show which messages have been seen.
User can mute a conversation to reduce notification noise.
Admin can moderate: view all conversations, delete inappropriate messages, mute disruptive users.
Conversation list shows recent activity and timestamps for easy navigation.
content.note: ⚠️ FEATURE-FLAGGED: This functionality requires 'messenger' feature flag to be enabled. May not be available.

content.summary: Investment portfolio management with real-time commodity price tracking.
steps: User opens commodities section to view available commodities (gold, silver, platinum, crypto, etc.).
User views current prices for each commodity with hourly updates.
To buy: user selects commodity, enters quantity and purchase price, submits order.
System validates: user has sufficient funds for purchase. Balance is deducted, commodity is added to portfolio.
Portfolio dashboard shows current holdings, purchase price, current value, and profit/loss.
User can check price history for trend analysis and investment decisions.
To sell: user selects holding, enters selling quantity, completes transaction. Balance is credited.
Financial transactions are created for all buy/sell operations.
Admin controls: can enable/disable trading, set maximum order quantities, and monitor market state.
content.note: ⚠️ FEATURE-FLAGGED: This functionality requires 'commodities' feature flag to be enabled. May not be available.

content.summary: Region-specific environmental monitoring for agricultural planning.
steps: User views weather dashboard showing current conditions for their region/district.
Weather data available for all 16 Polish voivodeships with current and forecast information.
User checks forecast for upcoming week to plan field activities, irrigation, harvesting.
System generates specialized alerts: weather warnings, irrigation recommendations, pest alerts, disease risks, soil conditions, machinery maintenance, livestock health, logistics notifications.
User can export weather data to PDF or CSV for records and analysis.
Alerts appear in real-time as conditions develop: severe weather, frost warnings, irrigation needs, etc.
Admin manages alert triggers and thresholds.
Historical weather data available for trend analysis and planning.
content.note: ⚠️ FEATURE-FLAGGED: This functionality requires 'weather' and 'alerts' feature flags to be enabled. May not be available.

content.summary: Build connections and manage relationships with other farmers.
steps: User views friend list and user discovery interface.
To add friend: user searches for another user and sends friend request.
Friend request is received and can be accepted or declined.
Once connected, users can see each other's public profile information.
Users can communicate via direct messaging with friends.
To block user: user can block another user to prevent communication.
Blocked users cannot send messages or see the blocker's profile.
Statistics show user engagement metrics and farm activity levels.

content.summary: Access farming knowledge and recommendations via AI chatbot.
steps: User opens AI assistance chat interface.
User types a question about farming,resources, financial decisions, or system features.
System queries knowledge base and uses LLM (Gemini, OpenRouter) to generate contextual response.
Response is delivered with recommended actions or information.
User can continue conversation with follow-up questions, maintaining context.
Responses are cached for common queries, improving performance.
Knowledge base includes complete system documentation for instant querying.
content.note: ⚠️ FEATURE-FLAGGED: This functionality requires 'assistant' feature flag to be enabled. May not be available.

title: Unique Aspects
content: Intentional bugs for debugging and test automation practice.
JSON database for simplified data control during testing.

title: API Basics
content: All main features are accessible via RESTful API endpoints (see API Documentation section).
Authentication is required for most endpoints (token in Authorization header).
Swagger UI and OpenAPI schema are available at /swagger.html and /public/schema/openapi.json for interactive API exploration.

title: Testing Tips
content: Use demo accounts to quickly access different roles and test permissions.
Test registration, login, and logout flows for all user types.
Verify role-based access: farmers should not access admin/superadmin features.
Test marketplace: create, buy, and approve offers as different roles.
Check financial operations: ensure balances update after transactions and transfers.
Try edge cases: invalid logins, insufficient funds, etc.
Use Swagger UI to test API endpoints directly.

content.description: Basic farmer with access to personal farm management.
Basic farmer with access to personal farm management.
Basic farmer with access to personal farm management.

content.heading: Overview
content: This section describes how assets (fields, animals), finances, and the marketplace interact in the system, including all rules, checks, and real-world scenarios.

items: User: Owns assets and a financial account.
Field: Land plot, can be assigned to staff or animals.
Animal: Livestock, can be assigned to a field.
Assignment: Links staff or animals to fields.
FinancialAccount: Tracks user balance and transactions.
MarketplaceOffer: Represents an offer to sell a field or animal.
Assets (fields/animals) are owned by users.
Fields can have staff or animals assigned (see assignments.json and fieldId in animals).
Animals can be assigned to fields (fieldId > 0).
Rule: Assigned/in-use assets cannot be sold or bought; their offers are marked 'unavailable'.
Ownership is transferred on sale.
Each user has a financial account with a balance and transaction history.
Transaction types: 'income' (money in), 'expense' (money out).
No overdraft: users cannot spend more than their balance.
User selects an asset they own.
System checks: asset is not assigned/in use and not already offered.
If valid, offer is created as 'active'.
Buyer selects an active offer.
System checks: not seller, enough funds, asset still available.
If valid: money is transferred, financial transactions are created, asset ownership is transferred, offer is marked 'sold', and a marketplace transaction is recorded.
active: available for purchase
sold: already bought
cancelled: withdrawn by seller
unavailable: asset is in use/assigned

Selling an Unassigned Field: User 2 owns 'Willow Grove' (field 22), not assigned. User 2 creates an offer (active). User 3 buys it: funds and assignment checked, balances updated, ownership transferred, offer marked 'sold'.
Attempting to Sell an Assigned Field: User 4 owns 'Birch Pasture' (field 25), assigned to staff. Offer is created as 'unavailable'.
Selling an Unassigned Animal: User 2 owns goats (animal 63), not assigned. Offer is 'active'. User 1 buys: funds and assignment checked, balances updated, ownership transferred, offer marked 'sold'.
Attempting to Buy with Insufficient Funds: User 2 tries to buy a field for 1000 ROL with only 420 ROL. System blocks purchase: 'Insufficient funds: overdraft is not allowed.'
Attempting to Sell an Assigned Animal: User 4 owns cows (animal 62), assigned to Birch Pasture. Offer is 'unavailable'.
Every sale results in: asset ownership transfer, financial transactions for buyer and seller, marketplace offer and transaction records.
No asset can be sold or bought if assigned/in use.
No user can spend more than their balance.
All references (userId, fieldId, animalId) are consistent across all databases.

content.description: A new user registers, logs in, and sets up their farm with fields, animals, and staff.
steps: Navigate to the registration page and create a new account with a unique username and password.
Log in with the new credentials.
Access the farm management dashboard.
Add a new field with a name and area.
Add animals (e.g., cows, goats) assign them to field.
Add staff members and assign them to field.
Review the farm overview to confirm all resources are listed.

content.scenario: Sell a Field on the Marketplace
content.description: A user creates a marketplace offer to sell a field, and another user purchases it.
steps: User A logs in and navigates to their farm dashboard.
User A selects an unassigned field and creates a marketplace offer to sell it.
User B logs in
User B browses the marketplace offers.
User B finds User A's field offer
User B initiates a purchase.
System checks User B's balance and field availability.
Ownership of the field is transferred to User B, and balances are updated.

content.scenario: Attempt to Buy with Insufficient Funds
content.description: A user tries to buy a resource but does not have enough virtual currency.
steps: User logs in and browses the marketplace.
User selects an offer that costs more than their current balance.
User attempts to purchase the offer.
System blocks the transaction and displays an 'Insufficient funds' error message.
User can view their balance and transaction history to understand the issue.

content.title: System-wide Feature Control
content.description: Runtime feature management without code deployment
items: Rolnopol uses a comprehensive feature flag system for controlled feature rollout, A/B testing, and operational management. Features can be enabled/disabled at runtime without code deployment. ⚠️ Note: Some core functionalities are behind feature flags and may be unavailable if disabled.

content.title: Available Feature Flags
content.description: Core and optional system capabilities
items: ⚠️ alerts - Regional and specialized alerts for weather, irrigation, livestock, machinery, and logistics
⚠️ commodities - Investment commodity trading and portfolio management
⚠️ exports - Export functionality for data in PDF and CSV formats
homepage - Homepage content and marketing features
⚠️ marketplace - Peer-to-peer marketplace for resource trading
⚠️ messenger - Real-time direct messaging with WebSocket support
⚠️ notifications - Event-driven notification system with multi-channel delivery
⚠️ weather - Regional weather data integration and forecasts
⚠️ assistant - AI chatbot assistance with LLM integration

content.title: Administrative Controls
content.description: Manage feature flags across different scopes
items: Scope: can be controlled at global system level, per-user, or per-instance
Reset: Admins can reset all feature flags to default state
API Endpoints:
GET /api/v1/feature-flags - View all feature flags
PATCH /api/v1/feature-flags - Update specific feature flags
POST /api/v1/feature-flags/reset - Reset all flags to defaults
GET /admin/feature-flags - Admin view of feature configurations
PATCH /admin/feature-flags - Admin management of features

content.title: Strategic Applications
content.description: Real-world feature flag usage patterns
items: Beta testing new features with subset of users
Gradual rollout of new functionality
Disable problematic features for incident response
A/B testing different feature implementations
Regional feature availability based on requirements

content.title: Resilience Testing Framework
content.description: Inject failures and monitor system behavior
items: Built-in chaos engineering controls for testing system resilience and failure handling.
Configurable chaos level: none, low, medium, high
Latency injection: introduce artificial delays
Connection dropping: simulate network failures
Partial responses: test incomplete data handling
Synthetic error generation: trigger exception handling
Per-endpoint configuration for targeted testing
API Endpoints: GET /chaos-engine, PATCH /chaos-engine, PUT /chaos-engine, POST /chaos-engine/reset
Usage: For testing application error handling, retry logic, and user experience during system faults

content.title: QA Support Tools
content.description: Simplify setup and execution of tests
items: Tools and features designed to simplify testing and quality assurance.
JSON database for easy data reset between tests
Database snapshot and restore functionality
Intentional bugs for test automation practice
Demo accounts for quick role-based testing
Feature flags for testing specific functionality paths
Mock data generation for consistent test scenarios
Repeatable test database states

content.title: Recommended Strategies
content.description: Proven QA testing patterns and approaches
items: Use demo accounts to test different user roles quickly
Enable/disable features via flags to isolate functionality
Create database backups before complex testing
Use chaos engine to verify error handling
Test edge cases: insufficient funds, assigned assets, invalid credentials
Verify financial consistency after transactions
Monitor logs during testing for unexpected errors
Use Swagger UI to test API contracts directly
Validate role-based access control across all endpoints
Test real-time WebSocket communication for messaging

content.title: Session & Token Management
content.description: Secure access with token-based authentication
items: Mechanism: Token-based authentication with session management
Token Storage: Authorization header 'Bearer <token>' for API requests
Cookies: 'rolnopolToken' for browser-based access
Login Time: rolnopolLoginTime cookie tracks session start
Session Expiration: Farmer users 24 hours, Admin users 1 hour
Logout: Clears authentication cookies and invalidates session

content.title: API Rate Protection
content.description: Resource protection and abuse prevention
items: Configurable rate limiting tiers to protect system resources and prevent abuse.
Tiers: auth (authentication attempts), api (general API usage), admin (administrative operations), high (critical operations), verify (token validation)
Configuration: Configurable per endpoint and tier via system settings
Protection: Prevents resource exhaustion and malicious requests

content.title: Hierarchical Permission System
content.description: Three-tier access control with granular permissions
items: Three-tier RBAC system with hierarchical permissions.
User (Farmer): Access to own farm and public marketplace
Admin: Full data access and moderation capabilities
Superadmin: Complete system control including configuration
Feature flags: Can further restrict feature access based on role

content.title: Asset & Data Safeguards
content.description: Comprehensive data integrity and user privacy protection
items: Assigned assets cannot be sold or transferred (marketplace safety rule)
Blocking prevents unwanted communication and profile access
Virtual currency balance protection (no overdraft allowed)
Transaction audit trail for financial compliance
User account deactivation for access revocation
Admin moderation of user-generated content (messaging)
